Thornhaven Capital plc (authorised by the Crown Financial Conduct Office) approves a limited cross-border export of compliance surveillance data to CloudHarbor Analytics Pte Ltd (Singapore) for model calibration purposes.
Export scope: trader chat transcripts and order-event metadata for Jan–Mar 2026 from Market Abuse Monitoring, with names pseudonymised to hashed TraderIDs and all attachments removed as a data minimisation control.
MNPI control: deal room materials concerning the proposed Rivermere Exchange secondary listing are excluded and redactions were completed on 20 May 2026; sample checks confirmed zero MNPI present.
Lawful basis: compliance with obligations under UK MAR and Thornhaven’s legitimate interests in market-abuse detection; the vendor will not perform automated decision-making with legal or similarly significant effects.
Supplier Onboarding Form FIN-SUP-09 and DPA Schedule 2 (Processor Instructions) are annexed, limiting processing to calibration and prohibiting profiling outside the documented purposes.
Transfer mechanism: the UK IDTA with CloudHarbor was signed on 18 May 2026, and a transfer risk assessment was approved by Ms. Priya Delmere (DPO) on 19 May 2026; encryption in transit and at rest has been verified.
This approval does not authorise any onward transfer, and the prior Asia-Pacific mirror pipeline was decommissioned on 30 April 2026.
DSAR: Mr. Edmund Kersey (Employee Ref THV-TK-1172) requested access on 08 May 2026; copies of relevant chat logs were provided on 15 May 2026 with counterparties masked and system audit trails included.
Consent withdrawal and erasure: Ms. Isla Penwright withdrew consent for optional wellness-programme data on 03 May 2026; related HR wellness fields were erased on 10 May 2026, with only non-identifiable aggregates retained for reporting.
Retention: vendor sandbox datasets are retained 90 days then auto-deleted; on-premise originals follow Policy DP-7.2 (12 months for surveillance chat; 6 years for payroll), with legal holds superseding where applicable.
Minimisation/segregation: no customer financial account data, bank details, or national identifier fields are included in this export; employee home addresses are not collected for surveillance purposes.
Security and access: access is limited to two CloudHarbor analyst roles (no named-user PII in exports), with per-record view logging and admin credential rotation completed on 18 May 2026.
The DSAR and erasure controls are operational; however, this memo alone does not create any right to delete trading records where statutory retention applies.
Cross-border transparency: the privacy notice was updated on 16 May 2026 and posted at https://thornhavencap.co.uk/privacy; contact privacy@thornhavencap.co.uk to exercise UK GDPR rights or raise questions.
UK staffing control: zero personal data from apprentices under 18 will be included; vetting confirmations for staff identifiers THV-AUD-001 and THV-AUD-004 were filed with Compliance.
Audit note: the previous control “US analytics bridge” is closed and must not be reinstated without a fresh assessment; no live transfers occur to the United States under this approval.
